subtitle: How a Bodhi Branch Crossed the Sea and Rooted Two Kingdoms in One Dharma

panels.shot: sweeping wide shot from above, sea between India and Sri Lanka, Mahinda and Sanghamitra on opposite shores
panels.action: Mahinda & Sanghamitta stand in serene profile on distant shores facing each other across a shimmering sea, hands joined in anjali, a stylized Bodhi tree canopy rising between them against a pale sky.

dialogue.text: In Ashoka’s age of stone and resolve, Mahinda and Sanghamitta carried the Buddha’s path and a Bodhi branch across the sea, binding Lanka and the Ganga plains in quiet dharma.

i18n.hi: अशोक के पत्थर और संकल्प के समय में, महेन्द्र और संघमित्रा बुद्ध का मार्ग और बोधि की डाली सागर पार ले गए, लंका और गंगा के मैदानों को शांत धर्म में बांधते हुए।

panels.shot: wide establishing shot of ancient Pataliputra from river level
panels.action: The wooden palisades and pillared halls of Pataliputra stretch along the Ganga, boats tethered at the bank as morning mist lifts over a sprawling Mauryan capital.

dialogue.text: Around 250 BCE, Pataliputra hummed with power. Ashoka’s empire stretched from the Hindu Kush to the Bay of Bengal, and the Ganga carried merchants, monks, and rumors of new reforms.

i18n.hi: लगभग २५० ईसा पूर्व, पाटलिपुत्र शक्ति से गूंज रहा था। अशोक का साम्राज्य हिन्दू कुश से बंगाल की खाड़ी तक फैला था, और गंगा व्यापारियों, भिक्षुओं और नए सुधारों की अफवाहें ढो रही थी।

panels.shot: interior medium shot in a royal hall, emphasis on Ashokan pillars and sober gathering
panels.action: In a pillared hall, monks in simple robes sit in ordered rows before an unseen throne, while Mahinda & Sanghamitta kneel toward the assembly, faces lit by shafts of light from a high lattice.

dialogue.text: Sri Lankan chronicles later spoke of a Third Council at Pataliputra, guided by the elder Moggaliputta Tissa, where doctrine was debated and missions to distant lands took shape.

i18n.hi: श्रीलंकाई ग्रंथ बाद में पाटलिपुत्र में तीसरी संगीति का वर्णन करते हैं, जहाँ महाथेर मोग्गलिपुत्त तिस्स की अगुवाई में doctrine पर बहस हुई और दूर देशों के लिए धर्मयात्राओं की योजना बनी।

panels.shot: medium close-up on Mahinda & Sanghamitta side by side, contemplative
panels.action: Mahinda & Sanghamitta sit cross-legged in a cloistered courtyard, Bodhi leaves flickering above; Mahinda’s gaze is steady and outward, Sanghamitta’s calm and inward, their shared posture serene.

dialogue.text: The texts call Mahinda a son of Ashoka and Sanghamitta his daughter, though later scholars debate whether those ties were of blood or dharma. What endures is their role as bearers of the teaching.

i18n.hi: ग्रंथों में महेन्द्र को अशोक का पुत्र और संघमित्रा को पुत्री कहा गया है, पर बाद के विद्वान मानते हैं कि यह रिश्ता रक्त से हो या सिर्फ धर्म से। जो बचा है, वह उनका धर्मवाहक होना है।

panels.shot: low-angle shot of Ashokan pillar with lion capital, Mahinda & Sanghamitta in foreground
panels.action: Beside an Ashokan pillar topped by a roaring lion capital, Mahinda & Sanghamitta stand with hands folded, listening toward the unseen emperor as courtiers and monks watch in still respect.

dialogue.speaker: Mahinda & Sanghamitta
dialogue.text: If the dharma is to live, it must walk beyond your frontiers.

i18n.hi: धर्म को जीवित रखना है तो उसे तुम्हारी सीमाओं से बाहर चलना होगा।

panels.shot: wide shot of a map-like floor painting, feet stepping toward Lanka
panels.action: On the polished floor of a hall, a painted map shows the Indian subcontinent and the teardrop shape of Lanka; Mahinda & Sanghamitta’s bare feet step softly toward the southern sea on the map.

dialogue.text: From this milieu, missions toward the northwest, the Himalaya, and the island of Lanka are remembered. In Sri Lanka’s chronicles, the voyage of Mahinda becomes the founding story of their Buddhism.

i18n.hi: इसी दौर से उत्तर-पश्चिम, हिमालय और लंका द्वीप की ओर भेजी गई यात्राएं याद की जाती हैं। श्रीलंका के ग्रंथों में महेन्द्र की समुद्रयात्रा वहाँ के बुद्ध धर्म की जन्मकथा बन जाती है।

panels.shot: medium shot on Mahinda in a forest, Sanghamitta in the background, departure mood
panels.action: In a forest clearing near Pataliputra, Mahinda & Sanghamitta stand beneath a large Bodhi tree; Mahinda looks toward the southern horizon, while Sanghamitta gently touches the rough bark, eyes closed in reverence.

dialogue.speaker: Sanghamitta
dialogue.text: Brother, the path we plant must be as enduring as this shade.

i18n.hi: भाई, जो राह हम बोएँ, उसे इस छाया जितना टिकाऊ होना होगा।

panels.shot: wide shot at river ghat, boats ready, monks embarking
panels.action: At a bustling Ganga ghat, simple river craft sway; Mahinda & Sanghamitta, with a small group of monks and nuns, step onto separate boats, turning once to bow toward the distant city.

dialogue.text: The tradition speaks of them traveling by stages southward. Mahinda’s path would lead to the rocky hill of Mihintale, Sanghamitta’s to a later voyage bearing a living branch of the Bodhi tree.

i18n.hi: परंपरा कहती है कि वे चरण-दर-चरण दक्षिण की ओर चले। महेन्द्र की राह उसे मिहिंतले की चट्टानी पहाड़ी तक ले गई, संघमित्रा की राह बाद की यात्रा में जीवित बोधिवृक्ष की डाली लेकर चली।

panels.shot: dramatic sea-level shot of boats on choppy water, sky vast
panels.action: Two wooden vessels ride the swells of a restless sea between India and Lanka; Mahinda stands at the prow of one, Sanghamitta seated in meditative pose on the other, both framed against a huge sky.

dialogue.text: Between the Coromandel coast and the northern shores of Lanka, their journeys crossed salt and wind. Later, these crossings would be remembered not as trade, but as dharma voyages.

i18n.hi: कोरमंडल तट से लंका के उत्तरी किनारों तक, उनका सफर नमक और हवा के बीच चला। बाद में इन पारियों को व्यापार नहीं, धर्मयात्रा के रूप में याद किया गया।

panels.shot: wide establishing shot of Mihintale’s rocky hill and forest below
panels.action: The rocky outcrop of Mihintale rises above thick forest, its stone ledges catching bright sunlight; small figures of hunters move below, while Mahinda & Sanghamitta are seen in the distance climbing a path.

dialogue.text: On the island, the chronicles place Mahinda at Mihintale, the ‘Mahinda’s hill’, where rugged rock meets forest. Here, they say, he met the king Devanampiya Tissa while the ruler hunted deer.

i18n.hi: द्वीप पर, ग्रंथ महेन्द्र को मिहिंतले में रखते हैं, ‘महेन्द्र की पहाड़ी’ जहाँ कठोर चट्टान जंगल से मिलती है। कहते हैं, यहीं राजा देवनम्पिय तिस्स से उनकी मुलाकात हुई, जब राजा हिरन का शिकार कर रहा था।

panels.shot: medium shot of Mahinda facing Devanampiya Tissa, Sanghamitta slightly behind
panels.action: On a sunlit ledge, Mahinda & Sanghamitta stand calm before a richly dressed Devanampiya Tissa with bow in hand; attendants and hunters halt mid-motion, caught between wariness and awe.

dialogue.speaker: Mahinda & Sanghamitta
dialogue.text: Great king, can this hill, rich with life, be hunted forever without end?

i18n.hi: महा राजा, क्या ये जीवन से भरी पहाड़ी यूँ ही अंतहीन शिकार होती रहेगी?

panels.shot: close-up on Devanampiya Tissa lowering his bow, Mahinda & Sanghamitta serene
panels.action: A tense close view shows Devanampiya Tissa slowly lowering his bow, eyes searching Mahinda & Sanghamitta’s tranquil faces, while distant forest glows green behind them.

dialogue.text: The Mahavamsa tells of questions and answers, of the king’s mind turning from sport to ethics. Whether every exchange is exact or not, the memory is clear: a ruler paused, and listened.

i18n.hi: महावंश प्रश्नोत्तर की कथा सुनाता है, जहाँ राजा का मन खेल से नीति की ओर मुड़ता है। हर संवाद सही हो या नहीं, याद साफ है: एक शासक रुका, और सुना।

panels.shot: medium group shot of monks teaching under a tree at Mihintale
panels.action: Under a broad tree at Mihintale, Mahinda & Sanghamitta sit teaching on stone slabs, scrolls laid beside them, as Devanampiya Tissa and his retinue listen cross-legged in respectful silence.

dialogue.speaker: Mahinda & Sanghamitta
dialogue.text: Dharma is not conquest. It is restraint, compassion, and a mind made clear.

i18n.hi: धर्म विजय नहीं है। यह संयम, करुणा और निर्मल मन है।

panels.shot: quiet interior shot of a simple shrine taking form, lamps lit
panels.action: Within a newly built stone hall at Mihintale, Mahinda & Sanghamitta oversee the placing of a simple altar; monks light small oil lamps as the king offers flowers, head bowed.

dialogue.text: Soon, according to tradition, monasteries rose. Mahinda became the teacher at the island’s first great sangha, and the royal house of Anuradhapura wove Buddhist rituals into the fabric of its rule.

i18n.hi: जल्दी ही, परंपरा के अनुसार, विहार उठ खड़े हुए। महेन्द्र द्वीप के पहले महान संघ के आचार्य बने, और अनुराधापुर के राजघराने ने बौद्ध अनुष्ठानों को अपने शासन की बुनावट में जोड़ दिया।

panels.shot: extreme close-up of the Rajagala inscription on stone
panels.action: A weathered stone slab at Rajagala bears faint Brahmi characters carved in neat lines, its surface dappled by sunlight filtering through surrounding forest branches.

dialogue.text: Centuries later, the Rajagala inscription, carved in early Brahmi, would quietly affirm that a monk named Mahinda was honored in Lanka, giving modern historians a hard trace beneath the stories.

i18n.hi: सदियों बाद, राजागल की शिला-लेख, जो प्राचीन ब्राह्मी में खुदा है, शांति से साबित करता है कि महेन्द्र नाम का एक भिक्षु लंका में सम्मानित था, जिससे आधुनिक इतिहासकारों को कथाओं के नीचे ठोस निशान मिला।

panels.shot: medium shot on Sanghamitta preparing a Bodhi sapling in a courtyard
panels.action: In an Indian monastery courtyard, Sanghamitta kneels beside a young Bodhi sapling in a clay vessel, carefully packing soil around its roots as Mahinda watches, hands folded in reverence.

dialogue.text: The chronicles then tell of a second, gentler voyage. Sanghamitta, now a central figure in this link, is said to have carried a cutting from the Bodh Gaya tree itself to the king of Lanka.

i18n.hi: फिर ग्रंथ दूसरी, मधुर यात्रा का वर्णन करते हैं। अब इस कड़ी की मुख्य नायिका बनी संघमित्रा के बारे में कहा गया कि वह खुद बोधगया के वृक्ष की एक डाल लेकर लंका के राजा के पास पहुँची।

panels.shot: wide shot of ship deck with Bodhi sapling, sea beyond
panels.action: On the deck of a sturdy ship, the Bodhi sapling stands cradled in an ornate planter, its leaves trembling in the breeze; Sanghamitta sits nearby in meditation, Mahinda standing at the rail watching the horizon.

dialogue.speaker: Sanghamitta
dialogue.text: A single branch can carry a memory across generations and oceans.

i18n.hi: एक अकेली डाली पीढ़ियों और समुद्रों के पार स्मृति ढो सकती है।

panels.shot: ceremonial wide shot in Anuradhapura, crowds and royalty around the sapling
panels.action: In Anuradhapura, robed monks, queens, and King Devanampiya Tissa gather in a vast courtyard as Mahinda & Sanghamitta stand beside the Bodhi sapling; the king pours water over its roots, watched by a hushed crowd.

dialogue.text: At Anuradhapura, the sapling was planted with ceremony as the Sri Maha Bodhi. Chroniclers would later guard its story, recording storms, invasions, and kings, always noting that the tree still stood.

i18n.hi: अनुराधापुर में, पौधे को विधि-विधान से श्री महा बोधि के रूप में रोपा गया। इतिहासकारों ने बाद में इसकी कथा की रक्षा की, तूफान, आक्रमण और राजाओं को दर्ज करते हुए, हमेशा लिखते रहे कि वृक्ष अब भी खड़ा है।

panels.shot: low-angle shot looking up through the Bodhi canopy, Mahinda & Sanghamitta silhouettes
panels.action: From beneath the Sri Maha Bodhi, the viewer looks up through dense heart-shaped leaves; in silhouette at the base, Mahinda & Sanghamitta sit in meditation, small against the living canopy.

dialogue.text: Today the Sri Maha Bodhi is honored as one of the oldest documented trees, its continuity recorded rather than cut for science. It stands as ritual memory of a moment when dharma crossed the sea.

i18n.hi: आज श्री महा बोधि को सबसे पुराने दर्ज वृक्षों में माना जाता है, जिसका अस्तित्व काटकर नहीं, लिखकर बचाया गया। यह उस पल की अनुष्ठानिक याद बनकर खड़ा है जब धर्म सागर पार कर आया।

panels.shot: wide contemporary-echo shot with modern pilgrims around the ancient tree
panels.action: In a timeless view, modern pilgrims in white and saffron circle the old Bodhi at Anuradhapura; Mahinda & Sanghamitta appear as faint, respectful figures in the background, watching the ongoing devotion.

dialogue.text: However shaped by later monks, Mahinda and Sanghamitta’s remembered mission transformed Lanka’s history. A peaceful journey of words and a sapling became a bridge of faith between neighboring lands.

i18n.hi: बाद के भिक्षुओं ने कथा जैसी भी गढ़ी हो, महेन्द्र और संघमित्रा का याद किया गया मिशन लंका का इतिहास बदल गया। शब्दों और एक पौधे की शांत यात्रा पड़ोसी धरतीों के बीच विश्वास की पुल बन गई।

title_i18n.hi: महेन्द्र का बोधि पौधे वाला सफर

subtitle_i18n.en: How a Bodhi Branch Crossed the Sea and Rooted Two Kingdoms in One Dharma
subtitle_i18n.hi: कैसे एक बोधि डाली सागर पार कर दो राज्यों को एक धर्म से जोड़े
